primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer si le revenu d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle est comptabilisé selon un droit d’accès à la propriété intellectuelle ou selon un droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: IFRS 15 is the only candidate governing standard in the context, and under the stated precedence rule IFRS standards govern the revenue recognition model for licences of intellectual property.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty because the context contains the specific IFRS 15 licence guidance that directly addresses whether revenue is recognised over time or at a point in time.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern the two licence models and the resulting timing of revenue recognition.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs establish the scope of licence guidance and clarify that the specific licence model applies when the licence is distinct.
These paragraphs clarify how to distinguish a right to access from a right to use.
These paragraphs provide the general revenue recognition consequences of over-time versus point-in-time satisfaction referenced by the licence guidance.
These paragraphs provide a royalty-specific overlay relevant to licence revenue but do not create separate peer top-level models.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address other over-time criteria, mainly for created assets and enforceable right to payment, not the licence model in question.
These paragraphs address progress measurement after an over-time model has already been selected.
These paragraphs address general variable consideration constraints rather than identifying the peer licence recognition models.
These paragraphs address significant financing components and presentation, which are not relevant to identifying the licence recognition approaches.
This agenda decision relates to tuition fees and is unrelated to licence revenue recognition models.

treatment_families.family: Distinct licence revenue models based on the nature of the promise in granting intellectual property rights

approaches.label: Right to access
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 identifies a licence promise to provide access to intellectual property throughout the licence period as a distinct top-level model.

approaches.label: Right to use
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 identifies a licence promise to provide a right to use intellectual property as it exists when granted as a distinct top-level model.
